Risk: Disabled accounts may retain access and can be re-enabled later with stale permissions.
Recommendation: Review disabled accounts monthly and remove group memberships during offboarding.

Risk: Missing attributes weaken lifecycle automation and governance scoping.
Missing job titles reduce audit and reporting quality.

Finding: Enabled users with no recorded sign-in activity
Risk: Never-used enabled accounts can represent stale or orphaned access.
Recommendation: Review and disable accounts with no legitimate usage.

Risk: Excessive access increases blast radius and violates least privilege.
Recommendation: Review memberships and move access into structured access packages.

Risk: Ownerless apps lack accountability for permissions, secrets, and lifecycle.
Recommendation: Assign owners and include apps in quarterly reviews.

Risk: Standing privileged roles increase compromise impact.
Recommendation: Move admins to PIM eligible assignments and review quarterly.

Risk: Disabled privileged accounts may regain admin access if re-enabled.
Recommendation: Remove all privileged roles from disabled accounts immediately.

Risk: Privileged access outside expected teams indicates role sprawl.
Recommendation: Remove inappropriate roles and enforce separation of duties.

Risk: Multi-role admins increase privilege concentration and separation-of-duties risk.
Recommendation: Reduce role stacking and use JIT activation.
